food_name: Intense Matcha and Mochi Lava Bun

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No specific FoodOn entry exists for matcha mochi lava bun; closest parent is sweet bread food product FOODON_03315091.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No FoodEx2 code exists for matcha-filled mochi bun specifically; A000E (Bread and similar products) is the closest defensible parent category.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT code identified for this specific filled sweet bun product.

serving.natural_language_portion: One matcha mochi lava bun weighing approximately 80 g, as typically served at a Chinese-style bakery in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Sweet bread roll, enriched — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 18350) adapted with matcha and mochi filling component estimates from USDA Foundation Foods glutinous rice and matcha powder entries
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for matcha mochi lava bun exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Values are estimated by component weighting: approximately 55% enriched sweet bread dough (USDA SR Legacy NDB 18350), 25% matcha cream filling (estimated from butter-cream base with matcha powder, USDA FDC entries 169562 and 173735), and 20% mochi filling (USDA FDC glutinous rice cooked, entry 168872). Macros blended proportionally. Sodium estimated from dough salt content. All values are estimates and should be verified against laboratory analysis.

gi_source.title: White bread and enriched sweet bread GI reference — University of Sydney GI Database; analogous estimate applied to matcha mochi bun
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for matcha mochi lava bun. GI estimated at 65 based on the University of Sydney GI Database entry for enriched white sweet bread (GI ~65, medium category). The mochi component (glutinous rice) has a reported GI of approximately 86 (high), but its proportion (~20% by weight) moderates the overall GI. The matcha cream filling contributes fat which further attenuates glycemic response. Blended estimate of 65 (medium) is considered defensible. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (65 × 38.8 g available carbohydrate per 80 g serving) / 100 = 25.2, rounded to 25.3.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (~168 mg per 80 g serving) and butter-enriched dough
hypertension.guidance: Individuals managing hypertension should limit consumption to one bun occasionally, as the sodium from salt in the dough and the saturated fat from butter may contribute to blood pressure elevation when consumed in excess.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High refined carbohydrate and sugar content with medium-to-high glycemic load (~25 per serving)
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should treat this bun as an occasional indulgence only; the combination of refined wheat flour, added sugar (~12.8 g per serving), and glutinous rice mochi produces a glycemic load of approximately 25 per bun, which can cause significant postprandial blood glucose elevation.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from butter and cream filling (~5.2 g per serving) and moderate cholesterol
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit intake of this bun due to its saturated fat content from butter and matcha cream, which may adversely affect LDL cholesterol levels when consumed regularly.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Intense Matcha and Mochi Lava Bun is a Chinese-style enriched sweet bread bun sold at RUXU (入续 糖水铺与包), a specialty bakery at Funan Mall in Singapore. It is made using the sponge dough method, which involves a two-stage fermentation process that produces a particularly soft, pillowy, and slightly chewy crumb. The dough is flavoured with high-quality matcha (green tea) powder, giving the bread a distinctive earthy, slightly bitter green tea aroma and a vibrant green hue. The bun is filled with two components: a rich, aromatic matcha cream that flows as a lava-like filling when the bun is freshly baked and warm, and a portion of homemade chewy mochi made from glutinous rice. The combination of the soft bread exterior, the molten matcha cream, and the elastic mochi interior creates a multi-textural eating experience. Nutritionally, each bun (approximately 80 g) provides around 254 kcal, with moderate fat from butter and cream (~9 g), significant refined carbohydrates (~38.8 g), and added sugar (~12.8 g). The glutinous rice mochi component contributes to a medium-to-high glycemic load (~25 per serving), making this a high-energy, high-carbohydrate treat. It is best consumed on the day of purchase. Clinically, this bun is a moderate concern for hypertension due to sodium and saturated fat, and a high concern for Type 2 Diabetes due to its glycemic load and sugar content. It is vegetarian but not vegan or gluten-free.

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional entry exists for this specific product in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Values are estimated by component weighting from USDA SR Legacy and Foundation Foods entries for enriched sweet bread, glutinous rice, butter cream, and matcha powder. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for enriched white sweet bread. Confidence is medium rather than low because the component sources are approved and the estimation methodology is transparent and defensible.
notes: Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the component source entries used for estimation (USDA FDC SR Legacy NDB 18350 and related entries). Selenium is -1 as it is not reported in the primary component sources used. Vitamin C is reported as 0.0; matcha powder contains trace vitamin C but the quantity in the bun is negligible and confirmed as effectively zero per USDA FDC matcha/green tea entries — confirmed zero by component analysis. Trans fat is estimated at 0.1 g per 100 g from butter content; this is a trace level. Halal status is listed as false as it cannot be confirmed from available public information for this specific restaurant product. The bun is best consumed on the day of purchase per the restaurant's own guidance. Nutrient values are estimates and should be verified against laboratory analysis or direct manufacturer data for clinical use. GI estimated at 65 (medium) based on analogous enriched sweet bread from University of Sydney GI Database; mochi component (GI ~86) moderates upward but fat content from cream moderates downward.
